body.purpose: Article prose; 1rem on 1.68 leading is the reading-room signature. Family inherited from the host theme.

code.purpose: Inline code and pre blocks on the dark code well; family inherited from --bs-font-monospace.

compact.purpose: Dense UI text: TOC links, card links, section stats, dialog paths (0.9375rem).

small.purpose: Metadata rows, breadcrumbs, leads, captions, footnotes (0.875rem).

meta.purpose: Secondary metadata, jump links, disclosure summaries, FAB labels (0.8125rem).

micro.purpose: Badges and drawer-tab labels (0.75rem); the only tier that may pair 0.04-0.06em tracking with uppercase, plugin-owned labels only.

subtitle.purpose: Chrome headings on the h3 step (1.3rem): index titles, letter headers, card headings, dialog titles.

subheading.purpose: Chrome headings on the h4 step (1.15rem): infobox titles, index subsection titles.

ornament.purpose: Decorative poetry-quote glyph (2.4rem, --wiki-prose-quote-ornament-font-family, Georgia serif fallback). Never for text.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Only for elements that overlap content: footnote popover, drawers, FAB dock, dialogs. Surfaces at rest are flat.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Every transition ships a reduced alternative; WCAG 2.2 AA commitment.

breakpoints.name: bootstrap
breakpoints.purpose: Bootstrap 5 grid breakpoints; the plugin adds no scale of its own.

components.name: Wiki Panel
components.description: Flat bordered surface for directory, sidebar, and manage panels.
components.html:
Recent changes

Flat surface, 1px border, no resting shadow.
components.css: .ds-wiki-panel { background: var(--wiki-chrome-surface-bg, var(--bs-card-bg, #fff)); border: 1px solid var(--wiki-chrome-surface-border, var(--bs-card-border-color, rgba(0,0,0,0.175))); border-radius: var(--wiki-chrome-radius, 0.375rem); padding: 1rem 1.25rem; color: var(--wiki-chrome-text-color, var(--bs-body-color, #212529)); } .ds-wiki-panel h3 { margin: 0 0 0.5rem; font-size: 1.1rem; color: var(--wiki-chrome-heading-color, var(--bs-heading-color, inherit)); } .ds-wiki-panel p { margin: 0; }

components.name: Redlink
components.description: Link to a missing wiki page; the classic wiki affordance, distinct from normal links on any theme.
components.html: See Uncharted District for details.
components.css: .ds-redlink-demo { color: var(--bs-body-color, #212529); } .ds-redlink { color: var(--wiki-redlink-color, #b04a4e); text-decoration: var(--wiki-redlink-decoration, underline dashed); text-underline-offset: var(--wiki-redlink-underline-offset, 3px); } .ds-redlink:hover { color: var(--wiki-redlink-action-color, #d06a6e); } .ds-redlink:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--bs-focus-ring-color, rgba(13,110,253,0.25)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Callout
components.description: Article callout panel: full border and icon well, never a side stripe.
components.html: Lore note: the plugin ships this slate default; themes retint via --wiki-callout-*.
components.css: .ds-callout { display: flex; gap: 0.75rem; align-items: flex-start; background: var(--wiki-callout-bg, #443b49); border: 1px solid var(--wiki-callout-border, #766f7d); border-radius: 0.375rem; padding: 0.85rem 1rem; color: var(--wiki-callout-text, #ece7df); } .ds-callout-icon { display: inline-flex; padding: 0.35rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; background: var(--wiki-callout-icon-bg, #332c38); color: var(--wiki-callout-text, #ece7df); flex: none; }

components.name: Footnote Popover
components.description: Floating footnote preview; one of the few shadowed elements.
components.html: 3 The scrim tokens were unified in the 2026 audit.
components.css: .ds-footnote-pop { background: var(--wiki-prose-footnote-popover-bg, var(--bs-body-bg, #fff)); border: 1px solid var(--wiki-prose-footnote-popover-border, var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6)); border-radius: 0.375rem; box-shadow: var(--wiki-prose-footnote-popover-shadow, 0 0.5rem 1rem rgba(0,0,0,0.15)); padding: 0.6rem 0.8rem; max-width: 22rem; font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--bs-body-color, #212529); } .ds-footnote-pop sup { color: var(--wiki-prose-footnote-marker-color, var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d)); margin-right: 0.3rem; }

components.name: Code Well
components.description: Dark syntax-highlighted pre block; self-contained on any theme.
components.html:
function resolvePath(segments) {
  // canonical title path
  return "/wiki/" + segments.join("/");
}
components.css: .ds-code-well { background: #201c26; color: #ddd6cb; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 0.375rem; padding: 0.9rem 1.1rem; font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.88rem; line-height: 1.55; overflow-x: auto; } .ds-tok-kw { color: var(--wiki-code-token-keyword, #d9b76f); } .ds-tok-fn { color: var(--wiki-code-token-function, #8fb7c9); } .ds-tok-var { color: var(--wiki-code-token-variable, #c9b8dc); } .ds-tok-cm { color: var(--wiki-code-token-comment, #8f8298); font-style: italic; } .ds-tok-str { color: var(--wiki-code-token-string, #9fca92); }

components.name: CSS Source Editor
components.description: The one custom input: a dark mono editing surface, overridable via --wiki-chrome-editor-*.
components.css: .ds-css-editor { display: block; width: 100%; background: var(--wiki-chrome-editor-bg, #111827); color: var(--wiki-chrome-editor-fg, #e5e7eb); caret-color: var(--wiki-chrome-editor-caret, #f9fafb); border: 1px solid var(--wiki-chrome-editor-border, #374151); border-radius: 0.375rem; padding: 0.75rem 0.9rem; font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.88rem; line-height: 1.6; resize: vertical; } .ds-css-editor::selection { background: var(--wiki-chrome-editor-selection, rgba(148,163,184,0.35)); } .ds-css-editor:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--wiki-chrome-editor-accent, #f472b6); outline-offset: 1px; }

narrative.overview: Sound structure, good light, empty walls: a reading room built to be furnished by whichever theme moves in. The plugin owns layout, hierarchy, and reading rhythm; it deliberately owns almost no color. Every visual decision routes through a three-tier fallback — var(--wiki-*, var(--bs-*, literal)) — so the same markup is complete on stock Bootstrap 5, on NodeBB Harmony, and fully re-skinned under nodebb-theme-westgate. The plugin must never reference a theme; themes opt in by setting tokens.
keyCharacteristics: Quiet and structural: nothing decorative; every element earns its border.
~100-token override contract in three families: --wiki-chrome-* (shell), --wiki-prose-* (article body), --wiki-redlink-* (missing-page links).
Flat surfaces; shadows only on things that literally float.
Reading-first: 1rem/1.68 prose, restrained heading scale, TOC and links tuned for lookup speed.

rules.name: The Three-Tier Rule
rules.body: Every color in plugin CSS is written var(--wiki-*, var(--bs-*, literal)). A bare literal color outside the owned dark surfaces is a defect. A reference to any theme-specific variable (e.g. --wg-*) is forbidden.

rules.name: The Tenant's Rule
rules.body: The plugin never sets a token a theme is meant to own; themes never need to touch plugin selectors — tokens only.

rules.name: The Author's Casing Rule
rules.body: Headings, TOC labels, and titles render exactly as written. No JS or CSS recasing, ever.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Rule
rules.body: If it doesn't overlap other content, it doesn't get a shadow.

rules.name: The Four-Tier Chrome Rule
rules.body: UI chrome text uses exactly four sub-body sizes - 0.9375 / 0.875 / 0.8125 / 0.75rem. A font-size between or below these steps is drift, not nuance.

dos: Do write every color as var(--wiki-*, var(--bs-*, literal)) — The Three-Tier Rule.
Do add a --wiki-chrome-* / --wiki-prose-* token whenever a theme could plausibly want to restyle a new element; ship it with a valid Bootstrap or literal default.
Do keep body prose at 1rem/1.68 and headings on the documented scale.
Do meet WCAG 2.2 AA: ≥4.5:1 body contrast, keyboard-operable drawers/dialogs/editor, prefers-reduced-motion alternatives.
donts: Don't reference theme variables (--wg-* or any theme's tokens) or theme class names in plugin CSS — the plugin must stay theme-independent.
Don't let wiki pages read as "a generic forum topic view": no post bylines, reply affordances, or thread chrome inside the article surface.
Don't build "heavy docs-site chrome": no persistent multi-rail app shell, breadcrumb ribbons, or toolbars everywhere.
Don't add "Fandom/wikia clutter": no ad-shaped modules, badge noise, or cramped content wells.
Don't recase headings or TOC labels; author intent survives.
Don't put resting shadows on panels, or side-stripe border-left accents on callouts and alerts.
